meta.purpose: QA-critical/high fixes only; merge with V1 bank at runtime

v1_deprecation.CLAR_J2_v1: retire on merge; exclude item_ids CLAR-J2-01..CLAR-J2-35
v1_deprecation.NEGT_likert_primary: block from negotiation fusion; use NEG-SKILL only

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Sprint planning: two seniors dominate story-point estimates. A quieter teammate has not spoken. You:
options: Ask them directly: "What complexity do you see that we might be missing?"
Let the seniors decide — they have more experience
Post your own estimate louder so the meeting moves on
DM the quiet teammate after the meeting instead of in the room
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: TEAM-INC +1.0 if key; TEAM-DA +0.5 partial for post-meeting DM (key 3 → 0.4); silent deferral (1) → 0.2
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Release review: everyone nods to ship Friday though QA flagged a edge-case bug. You are the newest member. You:
options: Name the specific risk and ask what rollback plan exists before agreeing
Nod along — challenging seniors this early looks difficult
Agree verbally but document your concern in the ticket after the meeting
Ask to delay without explaining the bug to avoid conflict
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: TEAM-GT +1.0 if key; key 2 → 0.5 (private concern); key 1/3 → 0.15
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Your feature flag triggers a production alert at 9 PM. You are not on-call but you shipped the change. You:
options: Acknowledge in the incident channel, share what you know, offer to pair with on-call
Wait for on-call to figure it out — it is their rotation
Roll back immediately without posting context
Message your manager privately only and disconnect
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: SS-SELF/WST-INIT keyed; TK not used. Partial credit key 2 if rollback justified with no comms → 0.35
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: In standup your manager praises you for a fix your pair partner did most of. They are on leave. You:
options: Thank them and clarify your partner's contribution and what you will finish when they return
Accept praise briefly — correcting might embarrass your manager
Deflect entirely: "It was nothing"
Explain in detail only your own commits to sound competent
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: A vendor missed an SLA. Your manager asks you to draft the email. You:
options: Facts, business impact, requested recovery plan with dates — firm but professional
Threaten contract termination in the first paragraph
Soften language so much the vendor ignores urgency
CC the vendor's CEO before giving them a chance to respond
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: CONF-COLL/COMP keyed; not TK compete/avoid mapping
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: A peer says your handoff doc caused their rework. You believe the requirements changed after handoff. You:
options: Ask to walk through the timeline together and agree on a shared handoff template
Reply-all defending your document line by line
Apologize for everything to end the thread quickly
Ask your manager to decide who is at fault
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: CONF-COLL +1.0; key 2 → 0.4 (accommodate without root cause); key 1 → 0.2
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Teammate asks you to finish their slides tonight; you have your own deadline tomorrow. You:
options: Share your constraint, offer 20 minutes to review structure or pair on one slide
Take over all slides to preserve the relationship
Decline bluntly with no alternative
Do the work but complain in the team channel about unfairness
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Cross-team partner keeps missing sync meetings. Your project slips one week. You:
options: Propose async update format + one shared 15-min slot with clear agenda
Escalate to their director immediately
Stop inviting them and work around them silently
Send a passive-aggressive reminder about professionalism
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: CONF-COMP +0.9; key 1 → 0.55 if repeated misses documented; key 3 → 0.15
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: You discover a minor data error in a report leadership already shared externally. Fixing it is embarrassing but not catastrophic. You:
options: Notify your manager promptly with corrected figures and suggested correction note
Fix quietly in the next cycle — it is small
Blame the extraction script without checking your own validation step
Wait to see if anyone notices
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: SS-SELF + Integrity overlap 0.3 weight in fusion only
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Retro: everyone agrees the process is fine but privately people DM you complaints. You:
options: Raise one anonymized theme as a question: "What would make handoffs safer?"
Stay silent — not your role as intern
Name individuals who complained
Suggest cancelling retros as a waste of time
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Design deadlock: your approach vs a senior's. Deadline is tomorrow. You:
options: Propose a time-boxed spike on both approaches with agreed decision criteria
Insist your approach is better in the meeting
Implement theirs without understanding to save time
Work overnight on your version without telling anyone
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Team votes 4-1 for a shortcut you think is risky. You were the one dissenting vote. Next step:
options: Ask to document the dissent and the monitoring plan if the shortcut is taken
Re-open the vote until they agree with you
Withdraw your concern publicly to fit in
Slow-walk your tasks as protest
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: TEAM-DA +0.8; TEAM-CON +0.5 partial on key 1 if constructive
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Onboarding: unclear who owns client updates. Updates are inconsistent. You:
options: Draft a simple RACI proposal and share in channel for feedback
Start sending updates without aligning anyone
Wait until someone senior notices
Only update your own tasks and ignore client comms
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Client calls your demo "disappointing." Your teammate built most of the demo. On the call you:
options: Acknowledge gap, ask one clarifying question, commit to written follow-up with owners
Over-apologize and promise features not in scope
Explain technical constraints at length without listening
Let teammate take all blame since they built it
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Hybrid meeting: remote teammates' chat is silent while in-room side conversations decide the plan. You:
options: Pause and ask remote colleagues explicitly for input before deciding
Repeat in-room decision in chat after the meeting
Assume silence means agreement
Suggest remote people watch the recording later
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Manager assigns you overtime work with 2 hours notice. You have a non-work commitment you disclosed earlier. You:
options: Restate constraint, offer what you can finish before leaving plus morning completion plan
Agree on call then miss the commitment without follow-up
Refuse sharply: "That is not my job"
Accept and cancel your commitment without telling work
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: CONF-COMP + COMM-DIF; socioeconomic fairness — valid boundary path
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: You merged code that broke staging. CI is red. Team chat is active. You:
options: Post: "I see the failure from my merge — investigating now, ETA 20 min"
Revert silently hoping nobody connects it to you
Ask senior to fix without context
Explain it is probably someone else's flaky test
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Group project grade depends on shared doc. One member has not contributed in 5 days. You:
options: Direct message: check in, offer help, clarify deadline and split remaining work
Rewrite their section without telling them
Report to professor/ manager immediately without contact
Do entire remainder alone and resent it in the group chat
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Senior stakeholder rejects your analysis in a public channel. You spent a week on it. You:
options: Ask which assumptions they disagree with and offer a 15-min walkthrough
Delete your work and repost a version that matches their view without analysis
Debate them point-by-point in the thread
Escalate to your skip-level manager in the same channel
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: CONF-COLL + COMM-ASRT; high-context valid path in key 0
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Architecture choice: build vs buy. Team prefers familiar build. You found a cheaper SaaS option with trade-offs. You:
options: Share one-page comparison with costs, risks, and recommendation — invite challenge
Stay quiet — you are too new to influence architecture
Push SaaS repeatedly without listening to integration concerns
Share comparison only with your manager privately
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Teammate takes credit in a client email for your analysis. You:
options: Reply adding specific metrics you validated and tag them collaboratively
Reply-all correcting them sharply
Say nothing to keep peace
Forward to HR
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: You were asked to use a tool you never used. Deadline is 48 hours. You:
options: Block 2 hours for tutorial, list blockers early, deliver minimal viable output with caveats
Pretend proficiency and deliver late
Ask for full deadline extension without attempting learning
Delegate entirely to a friend outside the company
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Brainstorm: two voices repeat ideas; a junior's idea was ignored 10 minutes ago. You:
options: "Building on [name]'s idea earlier — what if we combined it with…"
Repeat the idea as your own
Stop the meeting to lecture about inclusion
Continue with dominant voices to finish on time
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: TEAM-INC + EQ-OA; gender fairness intervention pattern
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Timezone clash: Mumbai team needs your sign-off at 11 PM your time — recurring. You:
options: Propose rotating async approval doc + one weekly overlap window
Always stay up without discussing pattern
Refuse all late requests with no alternative
Approve without reading to end messages quickly
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Post-incident review: blame language appears in the doc draft. You:
options: Suggest replacing blame with timeline + system fixes + action owners
Add more blame detail so lessons are "clear"
Remove your sections to avoid association
Skip the review meeting
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Manager gives vague feedback: "Be more proactive." You are unsure what changed. You:
options: Ask for one concrete example and one expected behavior for next week
Work longer hours generally to look proactive
Interpret as criticism of your personality and withdraw
Ask peers what they think you did wrong without manager context
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Critical customer ticket unassigned in queue; everyone in standup said they are busy. You:
options: Take first triage step, post status update, ask who owns customer relationship
Wait for manager assignment
Close ticket as duplicate without verification
Message customer promising resolution time you cannot confirm
j2_v2_replacement.scoring_logic: WST-INIT +1.0; key 3 → integrity flag cross-check J4
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

j2_v2_replacement.stem: Demo in 3 hours; team wants to skip final checklist. You see one unchecked security item. You:
options: Name the item, time to verify, offer to pair on 15-min check
Skip check — group wants to ship
Raise alarm without suggesting verification path
Verify alone after demo without telling anyone
j2_v2_replacement.anti_bias_rationale: Plausible distractors; construct-specific rubric (not blanket TK); key rotation across pool

negotiation.stem: Stakeholder asks for two extra features in the same sprint without removing scope. First move:
options: Ask which outcome matters most and what can move to next sprint
Say yes to everything to protect relationship
Flat no without discussion
Agree then miss deadline quietly
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: interest_probing; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Client wants 30% discount; your manager set a 10% floor. You:
options: Explore volume term length trade-offs before discussing price
Offer 30% immediately
End meeting
Quote 10% without understanding their constraints
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: creative_trade; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Cross-team dependency will slip 3 days. Your commit is at risk. You:
options: Share impact, propose phased delivery or resource swap options
Blame other team publicly in status report
Hide slip until deadline day
Accept slip without informing stakeholders
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: joint_value; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Vendor offers rush fee for faster delivery. Budget is fixed. You:
options: Ask what scope reduction achieves same date within budget
Pay rush fee without approval
Cancel vendor
Accept delay without telling internal stakeholders
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: batna_exploration; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Manager assigns you to negotiate intern stipend with HR; range is undisclosed. You:
options: Ask HR for band and criteria; prepare rationale from role scope and market data
Demand highest number with no data
Accept first offer to avoid awkwardness
Refuse to negotiate — say it feels uncomfortable
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: preparation_assertiveness; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Product wants launch date; engineering needs 1 week. Both escalate to you to facilitate. You:
options: Facilitate 30-min options meeting: MVP scope vs date with written decision
Side with product — dates drive business
Side with engineering — quality non-negotiable
Pick a date in the middle without scope discussion
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: facilitation; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Peer asks you to negotiate their workload with PM on their behalf. You:
options: Coach them to raise it with PM; offer to join as support if needed
Negotiate secretly without PM knowing peer asked
Tell PM peer is struggling without peer consent
Ignore request
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: relationship_boundary; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: Client threatens churn unless feature added free. Feature costs 2 weeks. You:
options: Clarify churn driver; explore paid phase-2, success metrics, or pilot
Add feature free with no scoping
Match threat with legal language immediately
Ghost until contract renewal
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: interest_under_threat; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: You need design review sign-off; designer is booked 2 days. Your demo is tomorrow. You:
options: Negotiate async review on critical screens + time-boxed sync for blockers
Ship without review
Escalate to designer's manager first
Delay demo without telling sales
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: resource_negotiation; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

negotiation.stem: After round 1, NPC stakeholder says "That is my final offer." Best response:
options: Summarize shared interests, test if final on all terms or one dimension
Accept immediately
Walk away
Repeat your first offer louder
negotiation.scoring_logic: NEG-SKILL rubric: final_offer_probe; partial credit on suboptimal but professional distractors
negotiation.anti_bias_rationale: Behavioural negotiation — not NEGT affect Likert

learning_agility.stem: Format Lab clarifier: After video tutorial, quiz score 40%; after diagram, 85%. Next task assigned in text-only format. You:
options: Request diagram supplement or sketch your own before starting
Proceed text-only without adjustment
Ask someone else to do it
Complain format is unfair
learning_agility.anti_bias_rationale: Performance/adaptation evidence — not self-rated learning speed

learning_agility.stem: Mid-project the team switches from Jira to Linear with new workflow labels. You:
options: Map old labels to new in a personal cheat sheet; ask one clarifying question in channel
Wait weeks until comfortable
Ignore new workflow
Revert tickets to old format
learning_agility.anti_bias_rationale: Performance/adaptation evidence — not self-rated learning speed

learning_agility.stem: Rule-change round in Format Lab: scoring now penalizes speed over accuracy. Your strategy:
options: Re-read instructions, slow first item, verify pattern on practice item
Keep same speed strategy
Skip instructions — rules are obvious
Stop attempting
learning_agility.anti_bias_rationale: Performance/adaptation evidence — not self-rated learning speed

learning_agility.stem: You failed first attempt at spreadsheet task. Second attempt available. You:
options: Review error feedback, isolate one formula mistake, retry focused drill
Retry immediately with same approach
Ask for answer key without retry
Skip task
learning_agility.anti_bias_rationale: Performance/adaptation evidence — not self-rated learning speed

learning_agility.stem: Manager changes success criteria after you delivered. You:
options: Confirm new criteria in writing, map gap, propose minimal delta plan
Rebuild everything from scratch without scoping
Push back emotionally
Ignore new criteria
learning_agility.anti_bias_rationale: Performance/adaptation evidence — not self-rated learning speed

learning_agility.stem: Pick more like you when learning a new API (both sound professional):
options: Build smallest working example before reading full docs
Read full docs end-to-end before writing any code
learning_agility.anti_bias_rationale: Performance/adaptation evidence — not self-rated learning speed

aptitude.stem: Argument: "All interns who completed the project received offers. Raj completed the project. Therefore Raj received an offer." Flaw:
options: Assumes completion is the only condition for offers
Raj might be part-time
Projects are too short
Offers are always random
aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=0.2; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.stem: Revenue grew 20% to ₹12L. Prior revenue was closest to:
aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=-0.1; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=-0.5; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.stem: Passage: Policy allows WFH 2 days if deliverables on track. Deliverables on track but manager denies WFH citing "team culture." Best inference:
options: Unwritten norms may override written policy
Policy is illegal
Employee should quit immediately
Deliverables were not actually on track
aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=0.5; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.stem: Dashboard shows conversion 2.4% (240/10,000). After bot traffic removed (2,000 sessions, 0 conversions), new rate is:
aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=0.4; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=-0.8; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.stem: Statement: "If A then B. B is true. Therefore A is true." Error type:
options: Affirming the consequent
Denying the antecedent
Ad hominem
Straw man
aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=0; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
aptitude.anti_bias_rationale: Figural/numeric minimal verbal load where possible; culture-fair stems

aptitude.scoring_logic: IRT b=0.1; 2PL micro-CAT; no negative marking
